segments.text: This is what the Sovereign Lord showed me:
He was preparing swarms of locusts after the king's share had been harvested and just as the late crops were coming up.
When they had stripped the land clean,
Sovereign Lord, forgive! How can Jacob survive? He is so small!
segments.speaker: Amos

segments.text: So the Lord relented.
This will not happen.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: This is what the Sovereign Lord showed me:
The Sovereign Lord was calling for judgment by fire; it dried up the great deep and devoured the land.
Sovereign Lord, I beg you, stop! How can Jacob survive? He is so small!
segments.speaker: Amos

segments.text: So the Lord relented.
This will not happen either.
segments.speaker: The Sovereign Lord

segments.text: This is what he showed me:
The Lord was standing by a wall that had been built true to plumb, with a plumb line in his hand.
And the Lord asked me,
What do you see, Amos?
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: A plumb line
segments.speaker: Amos

segments.text: Then the Lord said,
Look, I am setting a plumb line among my people Israel; I will spare them no longer.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: The high places of Isaac will be destroyed and the sanctuaries of Israel will be ruined; with my sword I will rise against the house of Jeroboam.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Then Amaziah the priest of Bethel sent a message to Jeroboam king of Israel:
Amos is raising a conspiracy against you in the very heart of Israel. The land cannot bear all his words.
segments.speaker: Amaziah

segments.text: Jeroboam will die by the sword, and Israel will surely go into exile, away from their native land.
segments.speaker: Amos

segments.text: Then Amaziah said to Amos,
Get out, you seer! Go back to the land of Judah. Earn your bread there and do your prophesying there.
segments.speaker: Amaziah

segments.text: Don't prophesy anymore at Bethel, because this is the king's sanctuary and the temple of the kingdom.
segments.speaker: Amaziah

segments.text: I was neither a prophet nor the son of a prophet, but I was a shepherd, and I also took care of sycamore-fig trees.
segments.speaker: Amos

segments.text: But the Lord took me from tending the flock and said to me,
Go, prophesy to my people Israel.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Now then, hear the word of the Lord.
Do not prophesy against Israel, and stop preaching against the descendants of Isaac.
segments.speaker: Amaziah

segments.text: Your wife will become a prostitute in the city, and your sons and daughters will fall by the sword. Your land will be measured and divided up, and you yourself will die in a pagan country. And Israel will surely go into exile, away from their native land.
segments.speaker: The Lord
